---
title: "Higher_Education"
author: "Anthony Zhong"
date: "2026-03-20"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Libraries and API key
```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(conflicted))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ipumsr))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))

conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter)
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::select)

set_ipums_api_key("59cba10d8a5da536fc06b59d19666ff1fc20409ba4fbe3c364f34079")
```

Extract Data
```{r}
usa_extract <- define_extract_micro(
  collection = "usa",
  description = "Recent college grads labor outcomes 2023-2024 ACS",
  samples = c("us2023a", "us2024a"),
  variables = c(
    # Technical / Weights (REQUIRED)
    "YEAR", "SERIAL", "PERNUM", "PERWT", "HHWT",
    
    # Geographic
    "STATEFIP",   # State (FIPS code) — for state-level maps/comparisons
    "MET2023",    # Metropolitan area (2023 delineations) — ~264 named metros
    "METRO",      # Metropolitan status flag — metro vs non-metro
    "REGION",     # Census region & division — Northeast, South, Midwest, West
    
    # Demographics
    "AGE",        # Age — filter to 22–27 for recent grads
    "SEX",        # Sex — gender breakdown
    "BPL",        # Birthplace — native-born vs foreign-born angle
    "CITIZEN",    # Citizenship status — naturalized, non-citizen, etc.
    "MARST",      # Marital status — married vs single earnings gap
    
    # Education (CORE)
    "EDUC",       # Educational attainment (general categories)
    "EDUCD",      # Educational attainment (detailed) — BA=101, MA=114, Prof=115, PhD=116
    "DEGFIELD",   # Field of bachelor's degree — THE key variable for by-major analysis
    "DEGFIELD2",  # Second field of degree — double majors
    "SCHOOL",     # Currently attending school — filter out current students if needed
    "SCHLTYPE",   # Public vs private school attended
    
    # Work
    "EMPSTAT",    # Employment status — employed, unemployed, not in labor force
    "LABFORCE",   # In labor force yes/no — simpler version of EMPSTAT
    "CLASSWKR",   # Class of worker — private, government, self-employed, nonprofit
    "OCC2010",    # Occupation (2010 harmonized codes) — what job they actually do
    "IND",        # Industry (current codes) — tech, healthcare, education, etc.
    "INDNAICS",   # Industry (NAICS codes) — standard classification, maps to BLS data
    "UHRSWORK",   # Usual hours worked per week — filter to full-time (35+)
    "WKSWORK2",   # Weeks worked last year (intervalled) — filter to full-year workers
    "WORKEDYR",   # Worked last year yes/no
    "LOOKING",    # Looking for work — context for unemployment
    
    # Income
    "INCWAGE",    # Wage & salary income — PRIMARY outcome (cleanest earnings measure)
    "INCEARN",    # Total earned income — wages + self-employment combined
    "INCTOT",     # Total personal income — all sources including investments
    "POVERTY"     # Poverty status — % of poverty line (100 = at poverty threshold)
  )
)
 
# --- Step 3: Submit, wait, download, and read — all in one pipeline! ---
 
# Option A: Full pipeline (submit + wait + download + read)
# This will take a few minutes while IPUMS processes your extract
acs_data <- usa_extract %>%
  submit_extract() %>%
  wait_for_extract() %>%      # Polls every 60 sec until ready
  download_extract() %>%
  read_ipums_micro()
 
# --- Step 4: Quick sanity check ---
cat("Rows:", nrow(acs_data), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(acs_data), "\n")
cat("Years:", unique(acs_data$YEAR), "\n")
 
# Quick look at education distribution
acs_data %>%
  filter(AGE >= 22, AGE <= 27) %>%
  count(EDUCD, wt = PERWT, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(10)
 
# --- Step 5: Save locally so you don't have to re-download ---
saveRDS(acs_data, "acs_recent_grads_2023_2024.rds")
 
# To reload later:
# acs_data <- readRDS("acs_recent_grads_2023_2024.rds")
 
 
# =============================================================================
# UNDEREMPLOYMENT ANALYSIS — How to classify "college-level" jobs
# =============================================================================
#
# The NY Fed method: if fewer than 50% of workers in an occupation hold a
# bachelor's degree, that occupation is "non-college." A BA holder working
# in a non-college job is "underemployed."
#
# You can compute this directly from your ACS data using OCC2010:
#
#   1. Use the FULL ACS sample (all ages, all education levels)
#   2. For each OCC2010 code, compute: share of workers with BA or higher
#   3. If share < 0.50, that OCC2010 = "non-college job"
#   4. Then filter to your recent grads and check which ones are in non-college jobs
#
# Example:
#
#   occ_college_share <- acs_data %>%
#     filter(EMPSTAT == 1, AGE >= 25, AGE <= 64) %>%
#     group_by(OCC2010) %>%
#     summarise(
#       pct_ba = weighted.mean(EDUCD >= 101, w = PERWT),
#       n = n()
#     ) %>%
#     mutate(college_job = pct_ba >= 0.50)
#
#   # Then join back to recent grads:
#   recent_grads <- acs_data %>%
#     filter(AGE >= 22, AGE <= 27, EDUCD == 101, EMPSTAT == 1) %>%
#     left_join(occ_college_share, by = "OCC2010")
#
#   # Underemployment rate by major:
#   recent_grads %>%
#     group_by(DEGFIELD) %>%
#     summarise(underemployment = weighted.mean(!college_job, w = PERWT))
#
# =============================================================================
 
 
# =============================================================================
# UNEMPLOYMENT FROM ACS — No CPS needed!
# =============================================================================
#
# ACS EMPSTAT codes:
#   1 = Employed
#   2 = Unemployed
#   3 = Not in labor force
#
# Unemployment rate = unemployed / (employed + unemployed)
#   i.e., filter to LABFORCE == 2, then weighted.mean(EMPSTAT == 2)
#
# Example:
#
#   acs_data %>%
#     filter(AGE >= 22, AGE <= 27, EDUCD == 101, LABFORCE == 2) %>%
#     group_by(DEGFIELD) %>%
#     summarise(unemp_rate = weighted.mean(EMPSTAT == 2, w = PERWT))
#
# =============================================================================

# =============================================================================
# TROUBLESHOOTING
# =============================================================================
#
# "Error: 2024 ACS sample not found"
#   -> The 2024 ACS was released Dec 2025. If IPUMS hasn't processed it yet,
#      just remove "us2024a" from the samples list and use 2023 only.
#      Check status: https://usa.ipums.org/usa-action/revisions
#
# "Error: Variable not available for sample"
#   -> Some variables aren't in all samples. The API will tell you which one.
#      Just remove it from the variables list.
#      Common issue: MET2023 might not be in older samples.
#
# "Extract is taking forever"
#   -> Large extracts can take 5-15 minutes. wait_for_extract() will keep
#      polling. You can also check status at:
#      https://usa.ipums.org/usa-action/data_requests/download
#
# "I want to check what's available before submitting"
#   -> Use get_metadata_catalog("usa") to browse available samples
#   -> Use get_metadata("usa", sample = "us2023a") for sample details
#
# IPUMS Special Codes (NIU = "Not in Universe"):
#   UHRSWORK: 0 = NIU
#   INCWAGE:  999999 = NIU (ACS top code also 999999)
#   INCTOT:   9999999 = NIU
#   POVERTY:  0 = NIU
#   OCC2010:  0 = NIU (not employed / no occupation)
#   WKSWORK2: 0 = NIU
#   Always filter these before computing stats!
#
# =============================================================================
```

```{r}
acs_data %>%
  filter(AGE >= 22, AGE <= 26, EDUCD == 101, LABFORCE == 2) %>%
  summarise(
    unemp_rate = round(weighted.mean(EMPSTAT == 2, w = PERWT),4)*100
  )
```
```{r}
occ_college_share <- acs_data %>%
  filter(EMPSTAT == 1, AGE >= 25, AGE <= 64) %>%
  group_by(OCC2010) %>%
  summarise(
    pct_ba = weighted.mean(EDUCD >= 101, w = PERWT),
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(college_job = pct_ba >= 0.50)

# Then join back to recent grads:
recent_grads <- acs_data %>%
  filter(AGE >= 22, AGE <= 27, EDUCD == 101, EMPSTAT == 1) %>%
  left_join(occ_college_share, by = "OCC2010")

# Underemployment rate by major:
recent_grads %>%
  group_by(DEGFIELD) %>%
  summarise(underemployment = weighted.mean(!college_job, w = PERWT))
```

```{r}
write.csv(acs_data, "IPUMS_ASC_Higher_Education.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
